title: SimPy queue simulation — verified Q&A
model_notes: Exponential interarrivals and service times; Resource capacity = clerks. Metrics match main.py: a customer is counted once they acquire a clerk (wait time ends). Utilization = sum of sampled service durations for those customers / (NUM_CLERKS * SIM_TIME).

questions.question: Using RANDOM_SEED 42, NUM_CLERKS 2, SIM_TIME 120, MEAN_INTERARRIVAL 4, and MEAN_SERVICE 6 (same model as main.py), how many customers begin service before the clock reaches SIM_TIME?
With RANDOM_SEED 7, NUM_CLERKS 1, SIM_TIME 90, MEAN_INTERARRIVAL 5, and MEAN_SERVICE 8, what is the mean queue wait (minutes) among customers who reach a clerk? Round to two decimal places.

With RANDOM_SEED 100, NUM_CLERKS 4, SIM_TIME 200, MEAN_INTERARRIVAL 3, and MEAN_SERVICE 5, what is the approximate clerk utilization as defined in the model notes? Give the value rounded to four decimal places.
With RANDOM_SEED 3, NUM_CLERKS 3, SIM_TIME 60, MEAN_INTERARRIVAL 10, and MEAN_SERVICE 4, what is the median queue wait (minutes) for customers who start service?
With RANDOM_SEED 99, NUM_CLERKS 2, SIM_TIME 150, MEAN_INTERARRIVAL 2.5, and MEAN_SERVICE 7, what is the longest queue wait (minutes) observed? Round to two decimal places.
With RANDOM_SEED 123, NUM_CLERKS 5, SIM_TIME 80, MEAN_INTERARRIVAL 6, and MEAN_SERVICE 3, how many customers begin service before SIM_TIME?

verification: Numeric answers were produced by running the same SimPy logic as main.py with the listed seeds and parameters (Python 3, simpy in .venv).
